title: Operation Dark Winter
summary: A 2001 senior-level tabletop exercise built around a fictional smallpox attack, and a documented real-world influence on the premise of The Division.

claims.text: Dark Winter was held on June 22–23, 2001 at Andrews Air Force Base and simulated a covert smallpox attack on the United States.
The exercise followed senior former officials through successive National Security Council meetings as the fictional outbreak expanded.
Ubisoft identifies Operation Dark Winter as one of the real-world events that directly inspired The Division's underlying premise.
The real exercise is an influence on the franchise, not an event inside The Division's fictional continuity.

sections.heading: What happened in the real world?

paragraphs.text: Dark Winter was a senior-level training exercise, not a real outbreak. Its fictional scenario tested how national leaders might respond to a deliberate smallpox attack while confronting vaccine shortages, intergovernmental coordination problems, public communication and civil-liberty questions.

Ubisoft explicitly names Dark Winter as a real-world inspiration for The Division. The useful comparison is the stress placed on institutions during a fast-moving biological crisis. The franchise turns that policy exercise into a fictional collapsed-world narrative; it does not reproduce the exercise literally.

comparisons.real_world: A fictional training scenario used in a real 2001 tabletop exercise.
comparisons.division: A fictional franchise in which a biological crisis causes institutional and social collapse.
comparisons.assessment: Documented influence, not an adaptation of the exercise scenario.

comparisons.real_world: Government decision-making, public-health capacity, communication and continuity under pressure.
comparisons.division: Field operations and survival inside the aftermath of a catastrophic outbreak.
comparisons.assessment: Shared crisis framework; different purpose and narrative scale.

verification.notes: All public claims are tied to primary or official sources. Human editorial approval is pending.
